lines.speaker: Junior Jim
lines.text: Alright, mate. What do you need?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'd like to buy a new boat.

lines.speaker: Junior Jim
lines.text: Sure thing, mate. Which one do you fancy?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'd like to customise a boat.

lines.speaker: Junior Jim
lines.text: Righto, mate. Just tell me which one you want to work on and I'll get everything set up.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'd like to recover a boat.

lines.speaker: Junior Jim
lines.text: No worries, mate! Just tell me which one you need and I'll get a port wizard to zap it right over.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'd like to destroy a boat.

lines.speaker: Junior Jim
lines.text: It's your boat's funeral, I guess.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Could I have another captain's log?

lines.speaker: Junior Jim
lines.text: Sure thing, mate.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Thanks!

lines.speaker: Junior Jim
lines.text: Not a problem. Don't forget to see Charles down in the cave on the island if you need more help with charting.

lines.speaker: Junior Jim
lines.text: Are you sure? Looks like you've got one already.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm sure.

lines.speaker: Junior Jim
lines.text: Fair enough, mate.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Thanks!

lines.speaker: Junior Jim
lines.text: Not a problem. Don't forget to see Charles down in the cave on the island if you need more help with charting.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Actually, never mind.

lines.speaker: Junior Jim
lines.text: No worries, mate.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Do you have any tips on how I can become a better sailor?

lines.speaker: Junior Jim
lines.text: Yeah, I reckon I can probably chuck a few pointers your way. Let's have a butchers...

lines.speaker: Junior Jim
lines.text: Right, you've got all sorts of ways you can buff up your sailing skills. There's port tasks, sea charting, shipwreck salvaging, Barracuda Trials, deep sea trawling, and probably loads more I've gone and forgotten about.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Can you tell me more about port tasks?

lines.speaker: Junior Jim
lines.text: Sure thing, mate. You'll find that a lot of ports will have a notice board where folks have gone and stuck up some ads for jobs they want sorting.

lines.speaker: Junior Jim
lines.text: Might be they want some cargo lugging about, or maybe they're after someone to pop out and do a bit of killing for them.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: And is there anything I should keep in mind while doing these tasks?

lines.speaker: Junior Jim
lines.text: Well, after you've gone and accepted a task or two, you'll want to keep your captain's log handy. It's good for keeping tabs on what jobs you're currently grafting away at.

lines.speaker: Junior Jim
lines.text: Also, if you're doing a bit of courier work, you might find it handy to get a couple of crewmates on board. They can help you shift the goods about so you're not knackering yourself by carrying one crate at a time.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Makes sense. Thanks, Jim.

lines.speaker: Junior Jim
lines.text: No worries, mate. Wanting any other tips?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Can you tell me more about sea charting?

lines.speaker: Junior Jim
lines.text: Well, mate, sea charting is all about heading out and improving your own knowledge of the sea itself. Your captain's log has all sorts of cracking tips on how to do so.

lines.speaker: Junior Jim
lines.text: If you want to know more, your best shout's going to be heading down into the cave on the island for a chinwag with Charles. He's the local expert on this sort of stuff.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I might just do that. Thanks, Jim.

lines.speaker: Junior Jim
lines.text: Not a problem, mate. Do you need any more tips?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Can you tell me more about shipwreck salvaging?

lines.speaker: Junior Jim
lines.text: Well, as the name suggests, mate, it's all about salvaging old shipwrecks from the sea. Keeps the place nice and tidy, and allows you to snag some swag from those that don't need it anymore.

lines.speaker: Junior Jim
lines.text: To get started, you're going to want to head over to the shipyard and sort yourself out with a proper nice salvaging hook. The flashier the hook, the quicker you'll be able to salvage.

lines.speaker: Junior Jim
lines.text: Once you've got your hook all nicely set up, it's time to head out and find a shipwreck. You've got plenty of different types to pick from. Once you spot a good one, just use the hook to get stuck in.

lines.speaker: Junior Jim
lines.text: You'll want to keep salvaging until you're feeling chuffed with your haul. Then you just need to use a salvaging station to sort through it all. Any half-decent port's going to have one.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I see. Thanks for the information.

lines.speaker: Junior Jim
lines.text: Always a pleasure, mate. Wanting any other tips?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Can you tell me more about Barracuda Trials?

lines.speaker: Junior Jim
lines.text: Sure can, mate. The Barracudas are a top group of daredevils, but they're also proper show-offs. They like to head out in their fancy boats, giving it proper beans and pushing them right to the limit.

lines.speaker: Junior Jim
lines.text: If you're a bit of a cheeky sod yourself, you might want to get involved in one of their trials. All of them are about speeding around and clocking the best time possible.

lines.speaker: Junior Jim
lines.text: Anyway, if you fancy it, the nearest one is just south of here in the Storm Tempor. Just don't go being a lemon and jumping in without a boat that's up to the task. Last thing you want is to go and sink!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'll be sure to keep that in mind. Thanks, Jim.

lines.speaker: Junior Jim
lines.text: No worries, mate. Need any other tips?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Can you tell me more about deep sea trawling?

lines.speaker: Junior Jim
lines.text: To be honest, mate, it's not something I'm properly clued up on. The basic gist though is that it's all about heading out in your boat to snag some fish that you wouldn't normally be able to get your mitts on.

lines.speaker: Junior Jim
lines.text: Still, you don't need to worry, mate. I may not be able to help you, but word is there's a geezer who can over in Port Roberts. Kellan is his name apparently.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I see. Thanks for the information.

lines.speaker: Junior Jim
lines.text: Not a problem, mate. Do you need any more tips?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Is there anything else I should keep in mind?

lines.speaker: Junior Jim
lines.text: Well, mate, you're going to want to keep taking full advantage of the shipyard. If you don't keep regularly upgrading your boat and the facilities on it, you're just holding yourself back for no good reason.

lines.speaker: Junior Jim
lines.text: Speaking of which, don't be afraid to sort yourself out with more than one boat. That way you can have different ones with different facilities on them. Do that, and you'll be golden regardless of what you're up to.

lines.speaker: Junior Jim
lines.text: Another thing to remember is that you don't need to go it alone. Get yourself a proper bang up crew, mate. They'll be able to help you with all sorts of things on your boat.

lines.speaker: Junior Jim
lines.text: Just speak to the Crew Registrar down the docks and she'll get you properly sorted out.

lines.speaker: Junior Jim
lines.text: Finally, mate, while self-betterment is ace and all that, don't forget to kick back, chill out, and enjoy yourself! Sometimes it ain't about the destination, it's about the journey!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Very true. Thanks, Jim.

lines.speaker: Junior Jim
lines.text: Always a pleasure, mate. Need any other tips?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Actually, let's talk about something else.

lines.speaker: Junior Jim
lines.text: No worries, mate.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Nothing, thanks.

lines.speaker: Junior Jim
lines.text: No worries, mate.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Junior Jim! How's it going?

lines.speaker: Junior Jim
lines.text: Calm down, mate. You seem cheery for someone out in the middle of nowhere.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What is this place?

lines.speaker: Junior Jim
lines.text: Well, mate. Some people like to call it Limbo... But I always thought that was the game with the stick.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Sure, but... Where are we?

lines.speaker: Junior Jim
lines.text: Somewhere out at sea. What does it matter? All the same, now you're here, isn't it?.

lines.speaker: Junior Jim
lines.text: Good luck with that, mate!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I want to get back to land.

lines.speaker: Junior Jim
lines.text: Alright, let's get you on your way then...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What are you doing here?

lines.speaker: Junior Jim
lines.text: Well, I thought it'd be nice to go on a little vacation. Get away from all the rabble.

lines.speaker: Junior Jim
lines.text: Didn't account for the rabble showing up here, did I?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Vacation? Is life on the Pandemonium all that taxing?

lines.speaker: Junior Jim
lines.text: Don't you go getting sarky with me, mate! Not like you ever work a hard day, is it?

lines.speaker: Junior Jim
lines.text: Can I help you, mate?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I don't think so.

lines.speaker: Junior Jim
lines.text: Okay... You should probably get going then.
